text: This is nice too - but I think there was something else.. <@UBKNXPBAB> wasn't there a cannonball demo in the programming tool whose name I forgot that you showed a couple of times? (something starting from A...?)

elements.text: This is nice too - but I think there was something else..
wasn't there a cannonball demo in the programming tool whose name I forgot that you showed a couple of times? (something starting from A...?)

text: Oh gosh – I always wanted to add a cannonball thing to Apparatus (as a test case for creating & destroying objects), but I never got around to that. Is it possible that’s what you’re thinking of?

text:
Greetings FoC,

Do you think the butlerian jihad has started or is getting some traction?

<https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47173121>

<https://www.anthropic.com/news/statement-department-of-war>

<https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47179553>

<https://docs.postmarketos.org/policies-and-processes/development/ai-policy.html>

I mean this in the sense of a more mature relationship with Technology? I recently listened to this:

<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kyak9MuzSsc>

The interview is very interesting, even if the host is a bit strange and self centered. They reach the point where they talk about LLM's and the host is like "its just too good" But then andrew mcluhan says this is my LLM ( points to his own brain) and this is my claude( points to his pencil)  Andrew is like a soft and calm voice trying to advocate for better relationship with technology. Check out his twitter for some funny slogans.  They briefly touch on privacy - its just too good. They also talk a few bits about <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/I._A._Richards#Feedforward> Its very interesting to see them , or at least Andrew talk about Meta Technology Reflection.  I felt  reminded of the snipped before the dune movie where they actually show the story of the butlerian jihad

<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=adV3BbO1Si4&pp=ygUZZHVuZSBtb3ZpZSAxOTg0IHByZSBzdG9yedIHCQm-CgGHKiGM7w%3D%3D>

What do you think?

text: Better not ruin my childhood memories by watching that again.  Saw a funny comment under some Dune themed video gen recently, "You realize all the characters in that scene would canonically murder you if they knew you used a computer to make it."
